channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Chad Chronister, Donald Trump’s pick to run the DEA, withdraws name from consideration
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Chad Chronister, Donald Trump's pick to run the Drug Enforcement Administration, said Tuesday he was withdrawing his name from consideration, becoming the second person selected by the president-elect to bow out quickly after being nominated for a position.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: UN human rights watchdog opens investigation into Venezuela presidential election
item.description: MIAMI (AP) — The top United Nations human rights watchdog on Tuesday ordered Venezuela to avoid destroying tally sheets and other electoral material as it investigates allegations that President Nicolás Maduro stole this summer's election. The U.N.
item.dc:creator: JOSHUA GOODMAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Justice Department announces sweeping reforms to curb suicides in federal prisons and jails
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Justice Department is ramping up efforts to address a crisis of suicides and systemic failures in federal prisons and jails, announcing sweeping reforms aimed at overhauling how mental health care is provided behind bars.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL BALSAMO and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UN watchdog to conduct probe into sexual misconduct allegations against top international prosecutor
item.description: THE HAGUE, Netherlands (AP) — A United Nations watchdog has been selected to lead an external probe into allegations of sexual misconduct against the top prosecutor of the International Criminal Court, The Associated Press learned Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: MOLLY QUELL and JOSHUA GOODMAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Jury deliberations begin in veteran Daniel Penny's trial over using chokehold on Jordan Neely
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Jurors began deliberating and soon revisited some of their legal instructions Tuesday in the trial of a military veteran charged with using a fatal chokehold to subdue a New York subway rider whose behavior was alarming other passengers.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: What happened to Hannah Kobayashi? Police say the Hawaii woman voluntarily disappeared
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Hannah Kobayashi, a 30-year-old woman from Hawaii who was reported missing in Los Angeles three weeks ago, walked freely into Mexico, police said. Authorities declared Kobayashi a “voluntary missing person,” meaning she left of her own free will.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jose Ibarra wants new trial after Laken Riley murder conviction
item.description: Ibarra was sentenced to life without parole and received additional prison sentences to be served consecutively.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Infant thrown from car during speedy Ohio pursuit; driver arrested
item.description: It took an hour for the infant to be found on the highway in below-freezing weather, according to court documents.
item.dc:creator: Lydia Reuille, NewsNation

item.title: California teacher dies after suspected bat bite in her classroom
item.description: A central California middle school teacher died after contracting rabies from a suspected bat bite, local health officials said.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, ABC 4

item.title: Family wants answers after FedEx truck hits dog
item.description: ELIZABETHTON, Tenn. (WJHL) - The Burleson family's pet dog Bear was injured after they say a FedEx driver delivered a package and then drove off, hitting the dog. Now, they're seeking answers and wanting an apology directly from the driver. Jessica Burleson adopted Bear 14 years ago.
item.dc:creator: Jayonna Scurry, NewsNation

item.title: Elon Musk's Neuralink expands offices in Austin area
item.description: Musk's brain-computer interface company, Neuralink, will expand in the area with more space and job openings.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Al-Shaikh, NewsNation

item.title: Trump says he would block US Steel acquisition by Nippon Steel
item.description: The Japanese firm announced last year that it would acquire U.S. Steel Corp. in a $14.9 billion deal — a move that was sharply criticized by lawmakers on both sides of the aisle.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Ocasio-Cortez first person to hit a million followers on Bluesky
item.description: The platform’s features are based off of Twitter, and they include the ability to message fellow users as well as a discover and feed tab.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Would-be Reagan assassin John Hinckley Jr. opening music store
item.description: Would-be Reagan assassin John Hinckley Jr. is opening a music store in Virginia, he announced to his followers on X.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Supreme Court doesn't seem convinced FDA was unfair in blocking flavored vapes as teen use increased
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A majority of Supreme Court justices didn't seem convinced Monday that federal regulators misled companies before refusing to allow them to sell sweet-flavored vaping products following a surge in teen e-cigarette use.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: President Biden faces backlash over pardon of Hunter and more top headlines
item.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trial date set for Brian Walshe, accused of murdering missing real estate executive wife
item.description: An October 2025 trial date has been set for Brian Walshe, who is charged with killing his 39-year-old wife and mom of three Ana Walshe on New Year's Day 2023.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Florida man accused of breaking into home, stabbing woman while she was sleeping inside
item.description: A Florida man was taken into custody after he allegedly broke into a woman's home, stabbed her while she was sleeping and attempted to flee from deputies.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Prosecution makes closing argument in murder trial over stabbing death of Cash App founder Bob Lee
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Closing arguments began on Monday in the trial of a tech consultant in the 2023 stabbing death of Cash App founder Bob Lee in San Francisco, an event that shocked the broader tech community whose members mourned the loss of an entrepreneur they called charismatic and kind.
item.dc:creator: JANIE HAR, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: California bill would give public university admission priority to slaves' descendants
item.description: California lawmakers are set to consider a bill that would require the state's public universities to give admission priority to the descendants of slaves.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIE AUSTIN, TRÂN NGUYỄN and OLGA R. RODRIGUEZ, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Unions score a major win in Wisconsin with a court ruling restoring collective bargaining rights
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — Wisconsin public worker and teachers unions scored a major legal victory Monday with a ruling that restores collective bargaining rights they lost under a 2011 state law that sparked weeks of protests and made the state the center of the national battle over union rights.
item.dc:creator: SCOTT BAUER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump warns of 'hell to pay' if Hamas doesn't release hostages
item.description: President-elect Trump on Monday threatened to unleash “hell” in the Middle East if hostages held by Hamas in the Gaza Strip are not released before his inauguration Jan.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, WGN 9

item.title: Cyber Monday shoppers expected to set a record on the year's biggest day for online shopping
item.description: Consumers in the United States are scouring the internet for online deals as they look to take advantage of the post-Thanksgiving shopping marathon with Cyber Monday.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO and WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Hannah Kobayashi's missed flight was 'intentional': LAPD chief
item.description: Kobayashi disappeared and stopped contacting her family just days after missing a connecting flight at LAX.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, KRON4

item.title: Biden pardons his son Hunter despite previous pledges not to
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden pardoned his son, Hunter, sparing the younger Biden a possible prison sentence for federal felony gun and tax convictions and reversing his past promises not to use the extraordinary powers of the presidency for the benefit of his family.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Biden displays AIDS Memorial Quilt at White House to observe World AIDS Day
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden on Sunday displayed the AIDS Memorial Quilt on the White House South Lawn for the first time in observance of World AIDS Day.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: FBI warns public of holiday-related scams targeting shoppers, donors
item.description: (KRON) -- Criminals are exploiting the holiday season to perpetrate scams against shoppers and donors, the Federal Bureau of Investigation San Francisco said, and people need to be vigilant. "Criminals don't take holidays off," FBI Special Agent in Charge Robert Tripp said in a press release.
item.dc:creator: Bay City News, KRON4

item.title: Lake-effect snow blankets the Great Lakes as Thanksgiving travelers head home
item.description: ORCHARD PARK, N.Y. (AP) — Lake-effect snow blanketed swaths of the U.S. as Thanksgiving travelers journeyed home this holiday weekend, with more snow expected to fall over the Great Lakes region through the week.
item.dc:creator: CARA ANNA, CAROLYN THOMPSON and JONAH BRONSTEIN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: What to know about Kash Patel, Trump’s pick to lead the FBI
item.description: Kash Patel has called for radical changes at the FBI and was a fierce and vocal critic of the bureau’s work as it investigated ties between Russia and Donald Trump’s 2016 presidential campaign.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER and ALAN SUDERMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Truck carrying 44,000 pounds of dried chickpeas catches fire in Death Valley National Park
item.description: A truck carrying 44,000 pounds of dried chickpeas caught fire in Death Valley National Park, according to the National Park Service.
item.dc:creator: Linsey Lewis, KTLA 5

item.title: Cucumbers sold in Utah, 25 other states recalled over Salmonella concerns
item.description: A produce company based in Arizona is recalling whole cucumbers that were distributed in more than two dozen states and Canada due to Salmonella concerns.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, ABC 4

item.title: Lake-effect snow heading for New York state could disrupt travel and football
item.description: BUFFALO, N.Y. (AP) — Black Friday was giving way to a white weekend in parts of New York state, with the first big snow of the season threatening to bury towns along lakes Erie and Ontario during a hectic holiday travel and shopping weekend.
item.dc:creator: CAROLYN THOMPSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: British lawmakers move closer to allowing terminally ill adults to end their lives
item.description: Members of Parliament approved the assisted dying bill by a 330-275 vote, signaling their approval in principle for the bill, which will undergo further scrutiny before it goes to a final vote.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Cryptocurrency entrepreneur who bought banana art for $6.2 million eats the fruit in Hong Kong
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — A cryptocurrency entrepreneur who bought a piece of conceptual art consisting of a simple banana, duct-taped to a wall, for $6.2 million last week ate the fruit in Hong Kong on Friday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4
